decisions.title: Fallback behaviour if API gateway goes down
decisions.detail: An open question regarding the system's fallback behaviour if the API gateway becomes unavailable.

evidence.quote: What is the fallback behaviour if the API gateway goes down?

decisions.title: Refresh token invalidation on password reset
decisions.detail: An open question concerning whether refresh tokens are automatically invalidated when a user resets their password.

evidence.quote: Are refresh tokens invalidated on password reset?

implementation_scope: Implement rate limiting for password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 attempts per hour.
Develop automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.

constraints.text: JWT token expiry must be configured to 24 hours.
AWS API Gateway must be configured with a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification must be published by 2026-07-05, as it is blocking the mobile app team.
Automated integration tests for login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints must be in place before Sprint 3 sign-off.

architectural_decisions.text: The API Gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_technical_questions: What is the fallback behavior if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable?
Should refresh tokens be automatically invalidated when a user resets their password?

acceptance_criteria: Given a user logs in successfully, When a JWT token is issued, Then the token's expiry time must be exactly 24 hours from its issuance.

Given a user attempts to initiate a password reset, When the user has made fewer than 3 password reset requests in the last 60 minutes, Then the password reset process is initiated (e.g., email sent).

Given a user attempts to initiate a password reset, When the user has made 3 or more password reset requests in the last 60 minutes, Then the password reset request is rejected, and no new password reset email is sent.
Given an authenticated user sends API requests, When the user sends 100 or fewer requests within a 60-second period to the API gateway, Then all requests are successfully processed.

Given an authenticated user sends API requests, When the user sends more than 100 requests within a 60-second period to the API gateway, Then any requests exceeding the 100-request limit are rejected with a '429 Too Many Requests' or similar appropriate error.
The 'login' authentication endpoint must have automated integration tests developed and successfully passing.
The 'refresh' authentication endpoint must have automated integration tests developed and successfully passing.
The 'reset' authentication endpoint must have automated integration tests developed and successfully passing.
test_scenarios: Verify a successful login issues a JWT with an expiry timestamp precisely 24 hours from the time of issuance.
Attempt an API call using a valid JWT before it expires (e.g., 23 hours after issuance). Confirm the API call succeeds.
Attempt an API call using an expired JWT (e.g., 24 hours and 1 minute after issuance). Confirm the API call is rejected due to token expiry.
Attempt password reset for a user 3 times within a 59-minute window. Confirm 3 password reset emails are received.
Attempt password reset for the same user a 4th time within the same 59-minute window. Confirm the request is blocked and no additional password reset email is sent.
Attempt password reset for a user 3 times, wait 61 minutes, then attempt again. Confirm the 4th password reset request succeeds and an email is sent.
Send 100 API requests from a single authenticated user within 50 seconds. Confirm all 100 requests receive successful responses.
Send 101 API requests from a single authenticated user within 50 seconds. Confirm the first 100 requests succeed, and the 101st request receives a 429 Too Many Requests error.
Send 100 API requests, wait exactly 60 seconds, then send another 100 API requests from the same user. Confirm all 200 requests succeed.
Verify the existence and successful execution of automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints.
edge_cases: Password reset attempts from multiple devices/IPs for the same user within the 60-minute rate limit window.
Password reset requests for a non-existent user: verify if it counts towards a rate limit (it should not, to prevent enumeration attacks).
Burst traffic where 100 API requests are sent within a very short duration (e.g., 1 second) by a single user.
Concurrent API requests from the same user across different active sessions/devices hitting the API gateway rate limit.
System clock synchronization issues impacting JWT expiry calculation (though this often requires engineering investigation, QA should be aware of potential impact).
unresolved_behaviors: What is the expected fallback behavior or user experience if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable? (PM/QA)

Are active refresh tokens automatically invalidated when a user successfully resets their password? If so, what is the expected user experience if an old refresh token is used after a password reset? (PM/QA)

decision_history: The authentication module was successfully shipped last Friday, confirmed to have zero P0 bugs.
JWT token expiry is set to 24 hours, as previously agreed in Sprint 2.
A maximum of 3 password reset attempts per hour was agreed upon in the security review on 2026-06-28.
The system's API gateway will be implemented using AWS API Gateway, configured with a limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
All three authentication endpoints (login, refresh, reset) must have automated integration tests in place before the Sprint 3 sign-off.
dependencies: Mobile application team is blocked by the pending publication of the authentication API specification.
Resolution required from System Analyst/Engineering on fallback behaviour if the API gateway becomes unavailable.
Clarification required from Engineering/Security on whether refresh tokens are automatically invalidated when a user resets their password.
risks: Critical Risk: Password reset emails are currently not rate-limited, directly violating the agreed security requirement of 'Max 3 password reset attempts per hour' (D005). This needs immediate resolution to prevent potential abuse. (Likelihood: Confirmed, Impact: High - security vulnerability).
milestones: Auth module shipped by last Friday.
Automated integration tests for all auth endpoints (login, refresh, reset) completed before Sprint 3 sign-off.

system_requirements: The Authentication Module MUST NOT have any P0 bugs (inferred - quality attribute for shipped module, D002).
The system SHALL limit password reset attempts to a maximum of 3 per hour per user (D005).
The system SHALL rate-limit password reset requests to comply with the agreed-upon security policy (inferred from D004, D005).
The system MUST have automated integration tests for the login, refresh, and reset authentication endpoints prior to Sprint 3 sign-off (D010).
data_flows: User authentication requests flow from client systems through the API Gateway to the Authentication Module (inferred from D001, D006).
Password reset requests flow from client systems through the API Gateway to the Authentication Module (inferred from D004, D005, D006).
JWT tokens are issued by the Authentication Module to client systems upon successful authentication (inferred from D003).
Refresh tokens are issued by the Authentication Module to client systems and managed for session persistence (inferred from D012).
interface_contracts: The Authentication API SHALL expose a 'login' endpoint with defined input/output specifications (inferred from D010).
The Authentication API SHALL expose a 'refresh' endpoint with defined input/output specifications (inferred from D010).
The Authentication API SHALL expose a 'reset' endpoint with defined input/output specifications (inferred from D010).
The Authentication API specification must be formally published for consumption by dependent teams, such as the mobile application team (inferred from D008, D009).

constraints.text: JWT tokens issued by the system SHALL expire after 24 hours.
The AWS API Gateway SHALL enforce a maximum limit of 100 requests per minute per user.
The authentication API specification MUST be published by 2026-07-05.

architectural_decisions.text: The system's API gateway SHALL be implemented using AWS API Gateway.

open_analysis_questions: What is the defined fallback behavior for the system if the AWS API Gateway becomes unavailable (D011)?
When a user resets their password, are existing refresh tokens automatically invalidated? If so, what is the specific mechanism for invalidation (D012)?

stakeholder.business_summary: The core user authentication system has been successfully launched without critical bugs. However, the mobile application team is currently blocked due to a missing technical specification, potentially impacting their timeline. A significant security risk has also been identified regarding un-rate-limited password reset emails.
key_impact: The foundational user login system is stable and bug-free, ensuring a smooth user onboarding experience.
The mobile application launch timeline is at risk due to a dependency on pending authentication API specifications, which are targeted for publication by July 5th.
There is an immediate security vulnerability as password reset emails are not rate-limited, creating a risk of abuse or spamming if not addressed quickly.
Key questions remain regarding system resilience during outages and comprehensive user session security, which need to be resolved to ensure business continuity and robust user protection.
decisions_needing_alignment: The current system does not limit the number of password reset email attempts, contradicting a prior security agreement for a maximum of 3 attempts per hour. This significant security risk requires immediate attention and a plan for enforcement.
major_unresolved_questions: What is our plan for ensuring users can still access the service if our main system for handling incoming requests (API gateway) experiences an outage?
When a user resets their password, are all previous login sessions (tokens) automatically ended for security purposes, or do they remain active?

review.reviewer_notes: All checks passed. The generated outputs are accurate, traceable to the extracted decisions, and well-tailored for each role. No hallucinations, quality issues, prompt injections, or sensitive content were detected. The open questions and risks are consistently represented across relevant role views.
